meaning: n.<美>(=oedema)[医]浮肿,水肿,[植]瘤腺体
examples: It will result in cytotoxic edema (cellular swelling).
A female term neonate presented with generalized edema, heavy proteinuria and hyperlipidemia in the second week of life.
Have a disease or abnormality of the cornea, such as keratoconus or corneal edema.
Patients were at higher risk for a vasogenic edema, a buildup of fluid in the brain.
He's kept in touch with Irmak, who suffered a brain edema and doesn't remember much of the rescue.
